name: Camera & Photo Capture
description: Access device camera for photo/video capture with preview and processing.
when_to_use: When app needs to capture photos; scan QR codes; or record video.
trade_offs: Permission handling is complex; must handle denial gracefully. Camera2 API is low-level.
implementation_notes: Android: CameraX (recommended) or Camera2. iOS: AVFoundation or UIImagePickerController. Compose: CameraX integration.
keywords: camera photo video capture camerax avfoundation

name: Location & Geofencing
description: Access GPS and network location with foreground and background tracking.
when_to_use: When app needs user location for maps; delivery tracking; or proximity features.
trade_offs: Battery drain from continuous location; background location requires extra permissions.
implementation_notes: Android: FusedLocationProvider + Geofencing API. iOS: CLLocationManager + CLCircularRegion. Always check permissions.
keywords: location gps geofencing fused clocationmanager background

name: Push Notifications (FCM/APNs)
description: Receive and display remote push notifications for user engagement.
when_to_use: When app needs real-time alerts; marketing messages; or transactional notifications.
trade_offs: Notification fatigue leads to opt-out; must provide value. Token refresh handling.
implementation_notes: Android: FCM + NotificationChannel. iOS: APNs + UNUserNotificationCenter. KMM: expect/actual for token.
keywords: push notification fcm apns token channel

name: Biometric Authentication
description: Use fingerprint; face recognition; or iris scan for secure authentication.
when_to_use: When app needs secure local authentication beyond PIN/password.
trade_offs: Fallback required for devices without biometrics; enrollment state changes.
implementation_notes: Android: BiometricPrompt API. iOS: LAContext (LocalAuthentication). Handle canEvaluatePolicy errors.
keywords: biometric fingerprint face id touch id authentication

name: File & Shared Preferences Storage
description: Store key-value pairs and files locally on device.
when_to_use: When persisting user preferences; tokens; or small cached data.
trade_offs: SharedPreferences/UserDefaults are not encrypted by default; use EncryptedSharedPreferences for secrets.
implementation_notes: Android: DataStore (Proto/Preferences) replaces SharedPreferences. iOS: UserDefaults + Keychain for secrets.
keywords: storage preferences datastore userdefaults keychain file

name: Runtime Permission Handling
description: Request and manage dangerous permissions at runtime with rationale dialogs.
when_to_use: Always when accessing camera; location; contacts; or other protected resources.
trade_offs: Users can deny permanently; must handle shouldShowRationale and Settings redirect.
implementation_notes: Android: ActivityResultContracts.RequestPermission. iOS: Info.plist + authorization APIs. Compose: rememberLauncherForActivityResult.

name: Bluetooth LE (BLE)
description: Communicate with BLE peripherals (wearables; IoT sensors; beacons).
when_to_use: When app connects to external hardware via Bluetooth Low Energy.
trade_offs: Connection management is complex; many edge cases (timeouts; disconnects; MTU negotiation).
implementation_notes: Android: BluetoothLeScanner + BluetoothGatt. iOS: CoreBluetooth (CBCentralManager). Handle background scanning limits.
keywords: bluetooth ble peripheral central scan connect gatt

name: Home Screen Widgets
description: Display app content on the home screen without opening the app.
when_to_use: When providing at-a-glance information (weather; calendar; fitness stats).
trade_offs: Size constraints; limited interactivity; platform-specific APIs.
implementation_notes: Android: Glance (Compose for widgets) or RemoteViews. iOS: WidgetKit + SwiftUI. Update via timeline.
keywords: widget home screen glance widgetkit remoteviews

name: Share & Intents
description: Share content to other apps or receive content from other apps.
when_to_use: When users need to share text; images; or files across apps.
trade_offs: Deep customization of share sheet is limited on both platforms.
implementation_notes: Android: Intent.ACTION_SEND + ShareCompat. iOS: UIActivityViewController. Share extensions for receiving.
keywords: share intent action send activity controller

name: In-App Purchases & Subscriptions
description: Integrate app store billing for consumables; non-consumables; and subscriptions.
when_to_use: When monetizing through paid features; premium content; or subscriptions.
trade_offs: Platform commission (15-30%); receipt validation is critical for fraud prevention.
implementation_notes: Android: Google Play Billing Library 6+. iOS: StoreKit 2. Always validate receipts server-side.
keywords: in app purchase billing subscription storekit

name: Accessibility APIs
description: Expose UI semantics for screen readers; switch control; and assistive technologies.
when_to_use: Always; for all public apps. Required by law in many jurisdictions.
trade_offs: Increases testing scope; custom views need manual semantics annotation.
implementation_notes: Android: contentDescription + Compose semantics. iOS: accessibilityLabel + accessibilityTraits. Test with TalkBack/VoiceOver.

name: App Links / Universal Links
description: Handle URLs that open specific screens in your app directly.
when_to_use: When enabling web-to-app navigation; marketing links; or push notification deep links.
trade_offs: Must verify domain ownership; handle fallback for uninstalled app.
implementation_notes: Android: App Links (verified) + intent-filter. iOS: Universal Links (apple-app-site-association). Both need HTTPS.
keywords: app link universal link deep link url domain
references: Android App Links; Apple Universal Links
